question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasConsumed field)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract call with a maximally-padded `functionParameters` byte array (all non-zero bytes) to inflate the intrinsic gas component of `gasConsumed` beyond the actual execution cost, causing the mirror node to report a falsely elevated fee that misleads downstream fee-auditing tools?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasConsumed field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call whose `functionParameters` consists entirely of zero bytes to minimize the intrinsic gas cost counted in `gasConsumed`, causing the mirror node to record a fee lower than the actual on-chain charge and breaking fee-consistency invariants?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasLimit field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a `gasLimit` value of Long.MAX_VALUE, causing arithmetic overflow in any downstream fee-estimation logic that multiplies `gasLimit` by a gas price, resulting in a negative or zero computed fee?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasUsed field)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where `gasUsed` stored in `ContractResult` is zero (e.g., via a failed Ethereum transaction path that sets `gasUsed(0L)`) while `gasLimit` is non-zero, causing fee-calculation consumers to compute a zero effective fee and bypass minimum-fee enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (amount field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call with `amount` set to zero while the actual HBAR transfer is non-zero, causing the mirror node to persist a `ContractResult` with a mismatched `amount` that downstream fee-reporting APIs expose as a free transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasConsumed field)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract creation transaction with a large `failedInitcode` payload to inflate the `TX_CREATE_EXTRA` (32000) plus per-byte intrinsic gas in `calculateGasConsumed`, causing the mirror node to record a `gasConsumed` value that exceeds `gasLimit` and triggers incorrect fee-refund calculations?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasConsumed field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HAPI-version branch in `updateGasConsumed` — where versions below the throttling threshold use manual calculation — by replaying an old-format transaction to force the legacy `calculateGasConsumed` path, producing a `gasConsumed` value that diverges from the actual charged fee?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasConsumed field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call where `gasConsumed` is null (no sidecar actions present) and `gasUsed` is non-null, causing fee-reporting endpoints to return inconsistent fee data that can be used to argue a transaction was free?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (payerAccountId field)] Can an unprivileged external user craft an Ethereum transaction where `payerAccountId` defaults to the Ethereum sender rather than the HBAR fee payer, causing the mirror node to attribute transaction fees to the wrong account and allowing the real payer to avoid fee accountability?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (senderId field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where `senderId` is set to a different account than `payerAccountId` via the `functionResult.hasSenderId()` path, causing fee attribution to be split or misassigned between sender and payer in downstream fee-audit queries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (transactionResult field)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a `WRONG_NONCE` or `DUPLICATE_TRANSACTION` result code that causes the importer to skip persisting a `ContractResult` entirely, making the mirror node report no fee for a transaction that was actually charged on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isNew()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `isNew()` always returns `true` to force a duplicate insert at the same `consensusTimestamp`, causing a constraint violation or silently overwriting an existing `ContractResult` record with manipulated fee fields?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasLimit field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with `gasLimit` set to zero, causing division-by-zero or undefined behavior in any downstream component that computes gas utilization ratio (`gasUsed / gasLimit`) for fee-efficiency reporting?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasConsumed field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call with a `functionParameters` payload that is exactly at the maximum allowed size, causing the per-byte intrinsic gas calculation (`TX_DATA_ZERO_COST * zeros + ISTANBUL_TX_DATA_NON_ZERO_COST * nonZeros`) to overflow a `long`, producing a negative `gasConsumed` value?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (amount field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call with a negative `amount` value (if the protobuf layer permits it), causing the mirror node to persist a negative HBAR transfer amount in `ContractResult` that downstream fee-calculation logic interprets as a credit rather than a debit?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toContractTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where `transactionResult` is set to a success code despite the contract execution failing, causing `toContractTransactionHash()` to persist a `ContractTransactionHash` with a misleading result that bypasses fee-penalty logic for failed transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasConsumed field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call with no sidecar records (making `totalGasUsed` null in `SidecarProcessingResult`), causing `calculateGasConsumed` to return without setting `gasConsumed`, so the mirror node stores null and downstream fee APIs report no gas cost for a transaction that consumed gas?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasUsed field)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a path where `gasUsed` is set from `functionResult.getGasUsed()` but `gasConsumed` is set from a different source (sidecar actions), causing a persistent mismatch between the two fee-related fields that confuses fee-auditing consumers?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (functionParameters field)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `functionParameters` byte array with a specific pattern of zero and non-zero bytes to minimize the computed intrinsic gas in `calculateGasConsumed`, causing the mirror node to underreport the actual fee charged for the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasConsumed field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract creation that fails (setting `failedInitcode`) with a large initcode payload, causing the `TX_CREATE_EXTRA` (32000) cost to be double-counted in `gasConsumed` if both the sidecar and the failed-initcode path are triggered simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (transactionNonce field)] Can an unprivileged external user replay a transaction with the same `transactionNonce` and `transactionHash` to cause the mirror node to record duplicate `ContractResult` entries with different fee values, corrupting the fee history for that account?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasLimit field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where `gasLimit` is much larger than `gasUsed`, causing fee-estimation tools that use `gasLimit` as a proxy for expected fee to significantly overestimate the cost and deter legitimate users from interacting with the contract?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (gasConsumed field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `gasConsumed` is a nullable `Long` to submit a transaction where the sidecar is present but malformed, causing `gasConsumed` to remain null while `gasUsed` is populated, creating an inconsistent fee record that bypasses gas-consumption validation?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (amount field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call with an extremely large `amount` value (near Long.MAX_VALUE) to cause integer overflow in downstream HBAR-to-tinycent fee conversion logic, resulting in a computed fee of zero or a negative value?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: ContractResult (payerAccountId field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a failed Ethereum transaction (non-success, non-DUPLICATE, non-WRONG_NONCE) to trigger `addDefaultEthereumTransactionContractResult`, which sets `gasUsed(0L)`, causing the mirror node to record zero gas used and thus zero fee for a transaction that was actually charged?
